seoTitle: How to Set Up a Commission Queue in Trello
metaDescription: Build an art commission tracking board in Trello with intake, sketch, and delivery stages, plus a shareable public progress view.
intro: Managing client waitlists through scattered direct messages makes it hard to keep commission work organized. This guide covers building a Trello commission queue with clear stages, from intake through final delivery, plus a way to share progress publicly.

steps.heading: Set up your status columns
steps.body: Build lists like Inquiry, Sketched, and Paid to represent each stage of your commission pipeline.

steps.heading: Use custom fields for commission details
steps.body: Add custom fields and labels to categorize commission types, pricing tiers, and usage rights.

steps.heading: Set board visibility
steps.body: Configure visibility settings to share a read-only progress queue with your audience without exposing private data.

steps.heading: Attach reference materials
steps.body: Add asset references, such as client concept folders and mood boards, directly to the card back.

steps.heading: Track invoice status
steps.body: Use labels or custom fields to mark whether a commission has been paid for.

steps.heading: Add quality checklists
steps.body: Use automated checklists to maintain consistent quality assurance steps across every piece.

faq.question: Can clients see my commission queue without a Trello account?
faq.answer: You can configure visibility settings to share a read-only view of your progress without exposing private data.

faq.question: How do I track pricing and usage rights per commission?
faq.answer: Custom fields and labels let you categorize each card by pricing tier and commercial usage rights.

faq.question: What stages should my commission board have?
faq.answer: A common structure includes Inquiry, Sketched, and Paid, though you can adjust it to fit your own process.

faq.question: How do I keep quality consistent across commissions?
faq.answer: Adding automated checklists to your cards helps maintain the same quality assurance checks for every piece.
